Apple

Technology

Artificial Intelligence

Supply Chain

Semiconductors

Building Supplier Ecosystems Where Demand Reveals Critical Manufacturing Needs, Government Investments Strengthen Capabilities, And Partners Become More Economically Resilient.

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image Credit: Justine Calma of The VergeMost big tech brands have their own way of working the system. Still, from the outside looking in, things seem as if Apple is playing catch-up in an attempt to prevent iPhone and Mac prices from going up more than they already are by giving into U.S government demands to put more of its manufacturing on home soil. You know the partnership with Intel and all. Providing them temporary relief from Trump’s Semiconductor tariffs.
Then again, Apple already had plans set in motion for a partnership with Intel to work on their entry-level silicon chips for iPhones and Macs, well before Trump’s demands of doing so in an attempt to reduce its enormous dependence on TSMC.
